translated: I would definitely have that on repeat.
Ah, it's the best.
Thank you, hair.
Seriously, it's so cool.
It's like, "Whoa!" It's amazing.
So cool.
Yabu-san doesn't remember it, though.
Yabu-san has no memory of it.
But it was just so beautiful,
I loved it. Really.
I'm glad.
That's good.
Everyone, please check it out. I'm sure some people are already saying that.
No, you really have to see it.
Please remember it, everyone.
Thank you very much.
Thank you.
Moving on, this one is from Liella name: Hajiketa Kata Popcorn.
Thank you!
Thank you very much.
Love Live! Superstar!! Liella! 7th LoveLive!
That was tough, wasn't it?
It really was.
All of us in the 2nd and 3rd generation were saying this,
but the choreography was incredibly difficult. It's the kind of difficulty
that's hard for the audience to imagine. From the outside, it looks like
we're having fun and dancing effortlessly, but it was actually brutal.
It really was tough.
Yeah, it was so hard.
When we first learned the moves, we all felt like our souls were leaving our bodies.
We were all worried if we could actually sing and dance it at the same time. But as we practiced, we got in sync, and the anxiety turned into enjoyment.
So on the day of the live, I felt more like I was enjoying the unity with the audience rather than being nervous. What did you think?
Yeah, definitely.
Right. After "High Motivation" was announced, so many people were excited to see the performance, which felt like a lot of pressure.
I actually thought the choreography would be a bit simpler or more relaxed.
I thought so too.
But there's so much dancing!
Wait, why is it like this?
Exactly.
I was worried we wouldn't be able to express the fun of the song. But as the live got closer, practice gave us confidence. We decided to just have fun and show the power of the 2nd and 3rd gens.
Right, right.
Yeah.
In the end, we had fun, but at first, we couldn't even think about that. We were just desperate. When we first tried singing and dancing together...
Oh, yeah.
We were all like, "This is impossible! This is seriously bad!"
Since there were fewer of us than the full 11 members, we couldn't even find a moment to breathe. The formations were so complex too.
And the rhythm wasn't just a simple 1-2-3-4 beat.
The choreographer had us hitting every tiny little sound in the music.
It turned out great, though. What part was the hardest for you?
Hmm...
Like Saku said, from the perspective of those watching, you're hitting every single beat.
But when you're just watching, you just end up thinking, "Wow, that looks fun!"
Yeah.
So, which part was actually the toughest?
With other songs, I can usually point to one specific part, but...
For this song, every single part was a little bit difficult, so it felt like it was hard the whole time.
I see.
Yeah, that makes sense.
It was just... tough.
Usually, there are scenes where you're not singing and you just stand there watching the others,
but there was almost none of that in this song.
You're always doing one or the other.
We were constantly moving. The formations were hard, but mostly it was just non-stop movement that left us breathless.
Yeah.
Right, right.
I can say it now, but I was a mess when we were first learning the choreo.
Really?
Is that so?
Seriously, everyone was crying while practicing.
I was practically sobbing while doing it.
I really wondered what was going to happen.
What's going to happen to us?
I thought Mei-chan would never cry, so seeing that made me cry even more. I don't even know.
Yeah.
No, no, that's not true.
Ah.
Well, I can talk about it now that it's over.
Everyone practiced so much at home. When we gathered again, the quality was on a whole different level. It was impressive.
Wait, there's even a line in the lyrics about "having changed," right?
Hearing this story now, I feel like that's exactly what it was about.
